parentQuestion: A patient complains that food has lost most of its taste, and you notice they ha...

scaffolds.q: Taste from the anterior tongue requires intact cranial nerve VII.
scaffolds.explain: The chorda tympani branch of CN VII carries taste sensation from the anterior 2/3 of the tongue. Embrace this:this — it's a small branch doing big work. Lose it, and flavor becomes muted, incomplete. Food loses its poetry.

scaffolds.q: The same nerve that controls facial movement also controls tear production.
scaffolds.explain: CN VII carries parasympathetic fibers to the lacrimal gland via the greater petrosal nerve. it controls both the actor's performance (facial expression) and the production crew (tears). Elegant multi-tasking.
